title: Contract Tests, Race and Leak Checks, Chaos, and Release · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs contract tests, race and leak checks, chaos, and release from producer, broker, queue, delivery, Go runtime, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one protocol or concurrency misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a production Go messaging defense dossier after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test pure policy, envelope schema, topology routes, publisher confirms, consumer effects, disposable broker integration, and production probes at distinct layers; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Inject return, nack, channel close, heartbeat loss, blocked connection, duplicate, poison, dependency timeout, majority loss, restart, stale generation, race, and goroutine-leak failures.

Roll out schema, topology, publisher, consumer, policy, and broker changes in a compatibility-safe order with shadow evidence and rollback; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Production Defense
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go messaging team handles case 8119 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate release on contracts, race and leak evidence, broker faults, compatibility, rollback, and upgrade path; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then make one state notification stale after recovery generation changes. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend architecture, contracts, live drills, capacity, security, dashboards, runbooks, rollback, recovery objectives, residual risks, and ownership before release; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Inspect the Production Defense trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Production Defense.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for defend architecture, contracts, live drills, capacity, security, dashboards, runbooks, rollback, recovery objectives, residual risks, and ownership before release; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend architecture, contracts, live drills, capacity, security, dashboards, runbooks, rollback, recovery objectives, residual risks, and ownership before release; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Production Defense, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Production Defense precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Production Defense, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go messaging team handles case 5786 during confirm-window load test with broker flow control. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate release on contracts, race and leak evidence, broker faults, compatibility, rollback, and upgrade path; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then make one state notification stale after recovery generation changes. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend architecture, contracts, live drills, capacity, security, dashboards, runbooks, rollback, recovery objectives, residual risks, and ownership before release; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Production Defense, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Passing unit tests and a local Docker demo proves production messaging readiness. In Go, passing go test and a local broker demo proves production recovery.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using defend and architecture plus specific case evidence.
